# Highlight

A component that highlights specific text within a string by applying custom styles to matching substrings. Useful for search results, keyword emphasis, and text annotation.

## Import

```jsx
import { Highlight } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `query` | `string \| string[]` | - | The text or array of text strings to highlight |
| `styles` | `object` | - | Style props to apply to the highlighted text segments |
| `children` | `string` | - | The full text string to search within |
| `ref` | `React.Ref` | - | Forwarded ref to the underlying element |

## Usage

### Basic

```jsx
<Highlight query="spotlight" styles={{ px: "0.5", bg: "yellow.200" }}>
  Put the spotlight on this text
</Highlight>
```

### Multiple query terms

```jsx
<Highlight
  query={["quick", "lazy"]}
  styles={{ px: "1", py: "0.5", bg: "orange.100", rounded: "md" }}
>
  The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
</Highlight>
```

### Search result highlighting

```jsx
const searchTerm = "react";

<Highlight query={searchTerm} styles={{ bg: "yellow.200", fontWeight: "bold" }}>
  Learn how to build applications with React and React Native
</Highlight>
```

### Custom highlight styles

```jsx
<Highlight
  query="important"
  styles={{ bg: "red.100", color: "red.800", px: "1", rounded: "sm" }}
>
  This is an important message for all users
</Highlight>
```

## Features

- Highlights matching text segments within a string
- Supports single or multiple query terms via string or array
- Custom styles can be applied to highlighted segments using Chakra UI style props
- Case-insensitive matching by default
- Non-matching text is rendered as plain text
- Forwards refs to the underlying element
